title: MIT License
text: U.S. Election Assistance Commission’s Election Administration Voting Survey (EAVS) work U.S. federal government subject copyright protection United States (17 U.S.C. § 105). package downloads EAVS data directly EAC may redistribute derived products. Users cite EAC’s EAVS Report Codebook relevant survey cycle using data; cite_eavs() returns recommended citation. package affiliated endorsed U.S. Election Assistance Commission.

title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: Start fresh R session (Session > Restart R, open new terminal R). Install GitHub user : Expect: installs without errors; startup messages warnings.

title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: eavs_cycles() Expect: tibble cycles 2016–2024 marked supported/verified earlier cycles marked planned. eavs_crosswalk() Expect: harmonized variable crosswalk tibble; skim rows sanity. cite_eavs(2024), cite_eavs(2024, "bibtex") Expect: sensible citation formats; BibTeX parses (stray braces, year 2024).

title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: eavs_cache_info() Expect: empty tibble (files expect earlier use; run eavs_clear_cache() first true cold start). download_eavs(2024) Expect: progress messages, file paths. Check files nontrivial size eavs_cache_info() (2024 data file tens MB, KB — tiny file means junk download). Run download_eavs(2024) . Expect: instant return “Using cached …” messages — re-download. download_eavs(2024, overwrite = TRUE) Expect: re-downloads replaces cached copy.

title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: eavs <- read_eavs(2024) Expect: tibble roughly 6,461 rows (2024 jurisdiction count) harmonized column names. Spot-check home turf: Expect: one Craighead County, AR row; eyeball registration turnout values know plausible. cb <- eavs_codebook(2024) Expect: codebook tibble; look one variable know confirm description matches EAC documentation.

what: 4. Classification edge cases (the part most worth your eyes)
title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: eavs <- classify_jurisdiction(eavs) Expect: new columns (jurisdiction_type, state_fips, county_fips), warnings can’t explain. Wisconsin (5-digit non-FIPS codes, county name parse): Expect: zero near-zero NA share; NA, look rows — multi-county cities lookup caught? Connecticut (2022+ planning regions): Expect: planning-region codes (09110–09190), retired 09001–09015 county codes. New England MCDs: pick NH VT town confirm county_fips matches digits 1–5 10-digit FIPSCode.
code: wi <- dplyr::filter(eavs, State_Abbr == "WI") mean(is.na(wi$county_fips)) dplyr::count(dplyr::filter(eavs, State_Abbr == "CT"), county_fips)

title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: county <- aggregate_to_county(eavs) Expect: roughly 3,100–3,200 rows (county equivalents); message documented behavior Alaska (dropped) territories. Conservation check — pick one count variable one state confirm nothing lost roll-: Expect: TRUE (explainable difference, e.g., dropped rows). Scan impossible values: negative counts, Inf, NaN aggregated table.
code: # replace `total_registered` with a real harmonized count column sum(dplyr::filter(eavs, State_Abbr == "AR")$total_registered, na.rm = TRUE) ==   sum(dplyr::filter(county, state_fips == "05")$total_registered, na.rm = TRUE)

title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: read_eavs(2022), read_eavs(2020), read_eavs(2016) — returns tibble plausible row counts (~6,400) harmonized names crosswalk covers . eavs_clear_cache(2016) removes 2016 files (eavs_cache_info() confirm).

title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: read_eavs(2030) read_eavs(2005) Expect: immediate, informative errors — download attempt. Graceful network failure: turn Wi-Fi, download_eavs(2020) (uncached cycle). Expect: clear “Download failed … EAC server may unavailable” message retries — , critically, eavs_cache_info() shows partial file 2020. Turn Wi-Fi back ; download works. Cache override: Expect: returns temp path, default cache.

title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: Visit https://cwimpy.github.io/eavsr/ — logo renders, reference index lists 11 exported functions, broken links. Read rendered vignette (“Get started”) site stranger — quick start actually run written? README GitHub: badges green, logo top-right, install instructions correct.

title: eavsr manual testing checklist (pre-CRAN)
text: win-builder emails (devel + release) report 0 errors, 0 warnings plus “New submission” note. rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck(args = "---cran") locally: 0 errors, 0 warnings. Working tree clean, pushed, CI green final commit. devtools::submit_cran() → click confirmation link CRAN email. acceptance: tag v0.1.0 GitHub, create release, link Zenodo, add DOI badge.

title: Getting started with eavsr
text: read_eavs() downloads cycle (already cached), reads tibble, recodes EAVS missing-value codes (negative integers -99) NA. download happens cached locally, later calls fast. read_eavs(level = "jurisdiction") (default) lower-cases column names janitor::clean_names() recodes missing values; level = "raw" returns file untouched. can also call download_eavs() directly want file path.
code: # One call: download (first time only), unzip, read, and clean. eavs <- read_eavs(2024)  # The file exactly as released, with no recoding or name cleaning: raw <- read_eavs(2024, level = "raw")

title: Getting started with eavsr
text: EAVS ships codebook describing ~500 variables. cycles whose codebook released spreadsheet, eavs_codebook() returns tibble (requires readxl package):

title: Getting started with eavsr
text: EAVS variable codes drift across survey cycles, makes multi-cycle work painful. eavs_crosswalk() returns hand-curated map raw EAVS codes stable harmonized name topical section:
code: eavs_crosswalk() #>    cycle variable                     harmonized        section #> 1   2024      A1a        registered_voters_total   registration #> 2   2024      A1b       registered_voters_active   registration #> 3   2024      A1c     registered_voters_inactive   registration #> 4   2024      B1a           voting_systems_count     technology #> 5   2024      C1a   absentee_ballots_transmitted    mail_voting #> 6   2024      C1b      absentee_ballots_returned    mail_voting #> 7   2024      C1c       absentee_ballots_counted    mail_voting #> 8   2024      D1a    polling_places_election_day polling_places #> 9   2024      D2a           polling_places_total polling_places #> 10  2024      D7a             poll_workers_total       staffing #> 11  2024       D8         poll_worker_difficulty       staffing #> 12  2024      E1a   ballots_rejected_provisional     rejections #> 13  2024      F1a  turnout_total_ballots_counted  participation #> 14  2024      F1b turnout_in_person_election_day  participation #> 15  2024      F1c        turnout_in_person_early  participation #> 16  2024      F1d                turnout_by_mail  participation #> 17  2024      F1e            turnout_provisional  participation #>                                             description #> 1                               Total registered voters #> 2                              Active registered voters #> 3                            Inactive registered voters #> 4                       Number of voting systems in use #> 5           Absentee/mail ballots transmitted to voters #> 6              Absentee/mail ballots returned by voters #> 7                         Absentee/mail ballots counted #> 8     Number of physical polling places on Election Day #> 9           Total polling places (Election Day + early) #> 10                         Total number of poll workers #> 11 Difficulty obtaining sufficient poll workers (scale) #> 12                         Provisional ballots rejected #> 13                                Total ballots counted #> 14               Ballots cast in person on Election Day #> 15           Ballots cast in person during early voting #> 16                        Ballots cast by mail/absentee #> 17                             Provisional ballots cast

title: Getting started with eavsr
text: EAVS reporting units vary state, — importantly — FIPSCode field. three patterns know : states use 10-digit code. trailing 00000 marks county-level row (county-reporting states); ending marks sub-county row (New England township states: , MA, NH, VT, CT, RI). Wisconsin uses 5-digit internal code Census FIPS code; ~1,850 municipalities carry county jurisdiction name (e.g. TOWN ABRAMS - OCONTO COUNTY). Connecticut reports town mapped nine planning regions. classify_jurisdiction() reads patterns adds jurisdiction_type, state_fips, county_fips, is_multi_county. keys state_abbr column (raw FIPS prefix), applies Connecticut Wisconsin name lookups needed. Autauga County (Alabama) county row; Massachusetts town sub-county row whose county_fips derived code; Connecticut town mapped planning region (09110). Connecticut Wisconsin lookup tables extensible — pass via ct_lookup wi_lookup arguments, see ?ct_planning_regions ?wi_subcounty_fips.
code: jur <- tibble::tibble(   fips_code         = c("0100100000",     "2500112345",   "0900112345"),   jurisdiction_name = c("AUTAUGA COUNTY", "EXAMPLE TOWN",  "ANDOVER TOWN"),   state_abbr        = c("AL",             "MA",            "CT") )  classify_jurisdiction(jur) #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   fips_code  jurisdiction_name state_abbr is_multi_county jurisdiction_type #>   <chr>      <chr>             <chr>      <lgl>           <chr>             #> 1 0100100000 AUTAUGA COUNTY    AL         FALSE           County            #> 2 2500112345 EXAMPLE TOWN      MA         FALSE           Sub-county        #> 3 0900112345 ANDOVER TOWN      CT         FALSE           Sub-county        #> # ℹ 2 more variables: state_fips <chr>, county_fips <chr>

title: Getting started with eavsr
text: aggregate_to_county() produces one row per county-equivalent FIPS code. county prefers directly reported county row; sub-county rows exist, sums numeric columns county. Ordinal questions (like EAVS d8) can summarized modal value instead summed. calls classify_jurisdiction() data classified. Alabama county carried directly (data_source = "direct_county"), two Massachusetts towns summed shared county (data_source = "aggregated_subcounty"). default U.S. territories Alaska dropped, since roll cleanly county FIPS; see ?aggregate_to_county change .
code: example <- tibble::tibble(   fips_code         = c("0100100000",     "2500112345", "2500167890"),   jurisdiction_name = c("AUTAUGA COUNTY", "TOWN A",      "TOWN B"),   state_abbr        = c("AL",             "MA",          "MA"),   total_voters      = c(40000,            12000,         8000) )  aggregate_to_county(example) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   fips_code  jurisdiction_name state_abbr total_voters is_multi_county #>   <chr>      <chr>             <chr>             <dbl> <lgl>           #> 1 0100100000 AUTAUGA COUNTY    AL                40000 FALSE           #> 2 2500112345 TOWN A            MA                20000 FALSE           #> # ℹ 5 more variables: jurisdiction_type <chr>, state_fips <chr>, #> #   county_fips <chr>, data_source <chr>, n_subjurisdictions <int>

title: Getting started with eavsr
text: EAC asks researchers cite EAVS release. cite_eavs() builds recommended citation text, BibTeX, structured list:
code: cite_eavs(2024) #> [1] "U.S. Election Assistance Commission (2025). 2024 Election Administration and Voting Survey Comprehensive Report. U.S. Election Assistance Commission, Washington, DC. https://www.eac.gov/research-and-data/studies-and-reports" cat(cite_eavs(2024, style = "bibtex")) #> @techreport{eac_eavs_2024, #>   author    = {{U.S. Election Assistance Commission}}, #>   title     = {{2024 Election Administration and Voting Survey Comprehensive Report}}, #>   institution = {{U.S. Election Assistance Commission}}, #>   address   = {Washington, DC}, #>   year      = {2025}, #>   url       = {https://www.eac.gov/research-and-data/studies-and-reports} #> }

title: Getting started with eavsr
text: Downloaded files live tools::R_user_dir("eavsr", "cache"). Override location EAVSR_CACHE_DIR environment variable.
code: eavs_cache_dir()    # where files are stored eavs_cache_info()   # what is currently cached eavs_clear_cache()  # remove cached files

what: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey
title: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey
text: eavsr tidy R interface U.S. Election Assistance Commission’s Election Administration Voting Survey (EAVS). downloads caches public-release EAVS files, harmonizes variable names across survey cycles, exposes codebook tibble, provides citation helpers. EAVS largest public dataset local election administration United States, covering thousands reporting jurisdictions (6,461 2024) across registration, voting technology, polling places, staffing, mail/absentee voting, ballot rejections, turnout. eavsr designed make multi-cycle analyses straightforward without rewriting variable-selection logic time. affiliated endorsed U.S. Election Assistance Commission.

title: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey
Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey
text: guided walkthrough, see vignette("eavsr").
code: library(eavsr)  # What cycles does the package know about? eavs_cycles()  # Download and read the 2024 cycle (cached on first download). eavs <- read_eavs(2024)  # Inspect the codebook as a tibble. cb <- eavs_codebook(2024)  # See the harmonized variable crosswalk across cycles. eavs_crosswalk()  # Classify each jurisdiction (jurisdiction_type, state_fips, county_fips). # Handles CT planning regions, WI sub-county lookups, and multi-county rows. eavs <- classify_jurisdiction(eavs)  # Roll up to county-equivalent geography (calls classify_jurisdiction() if needed). county <- aggregate_to_county(eavs)  # Get a citation (also "bibtex" or "list"). cite_eavs(2024)

title: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey
text: Downloaded files cached tools::R_user_dir("eavsr", "cache"). Override location EAVSR_CACHE_DIR environment variable. Inspect clear cache eavs_cache_info() eavs_clear_cache().

title: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey
text: EAVS public-release files works U.S. federal government public domain (17 U.S.C. § 105). EAC requests citation EAVS Report Codebook data used. cite_eavs(cycle) returns recommended citation text BibTeX form.

title: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey
text: rurality — county-level rurality classifications (RUCC, RUCA, IRR, OMB) pair naturally EAVS rural–urban analyses. rurality-stata — classifications Stata users.

title: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey
text: Current (v0.1): Download, cache, read 2016–2024 cycles verified EAC URLs. Codebook access, citation helpers, starter cross-cycle crosswalk. County-equivalent aggregation, including CT planning regions, WI sub-county name lookups, multi-county jurisdictions. Planned: Earlier cycles (2004–2014) added public-release URLs verified. Expanded, community-reviewed variable crosswalk. Multi-cycle harmonized Parquet bundle distributed via GitHub Releases piggyback.

title: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey

what: Aggregate EAVS to county level — aggregate_to_county
title: Aggregate EAVS to county level — aggregate_to_county
text: Combines EAVS rows reported directly county level sub-county rows summed parent county-equivalent geography. State-level multi-county rows dropped. Optionally drops U.S. territories Alaska, lack standard county-equivalent reporting.

title: Aggregate EAVS to county level — aggregate_to_county
code: aggregate_to_county(   df,   mode_cols = "d8",   numeric_fn = function(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE),   drop_territories = TRUE,   drop_alaska = TRUE )

title: Aggregate EAVS to county level — aggregate_to_county
text: df EAVS data frame. already classified, classify_jurisdiction() called . mode_cols Character vector column names summarized modal value instead sum. Defaults "d8". numeric_fn Function applied numeric columns sub-county aggregation. Defaults sum(x, na.rm = TRUE). drop_territories Logical. TRUE (default), drop , GU, MP, PR, VI sub-county aggregation step. Direct-county rows also dropped. drop_alaska Logical. TRUE (default), drop AK sub-county aggregation. AK reports borough level, roll cleanly county FIPS.

title: Aggregate EAVS to county level — aggregate_to_county
text: tibble one row per county-equivalent FIPS code, plus added data_source column ("direct_county" "aggregated_subcounty").

title: Aggregate EAVS to county level — aggregate_to_county
text: county direct EAVS row, row preferred aggregated sub-county sum. Sub-county aggregation uses sum(..., na.rm = TRUE) numeric columns default; specific columns can use different summary via mode_cols (modal value, useful ordinal questions like EAVS D8) numeric_fn (override default summarizer).

title: Aggregate EAVS to county level — aggregate_to_county

what: Citation for an EAVS cycle — cite_eavs
title: Citation for an EAVS cycle — cite_eavs
text: Returns properly formatted citation EAVS cycle's report codebook. EAC requests researchers cite underlying EAVS release; helper builds recommended citation string.

title: Citation for an EAVS cycle — cite_eavs
Citation for an EAVS cycle — cite_eavs
text: cycle Integer year EAVS cycle. style One "text" (single citation string, default), "bibtex", "list" (structured list fields).

title: Citation for an EAVS cycle — cite_eavs
text: character string (list, style = "list").

title: Citation for an EAVS cycle — cite_eavs
code:
cite_eavs(2024) #> [1] "U.S. Election Assistance Commission (2025). 2024 Election Administration and Voting Survey Comprehensive Report. U.S. Election Assistance Commission, Washington, DC. https://www.eac.gov/research-and-data/studies-and-reports" cite_eavs(2024, style = "bibtex") #> [1] "@techreport{eac_eavs_2024,
  author    = {{U.S. Election Assistance Commission}},
  title     = {{2024 Election Administration and Voting Survey Comprehensive Report}},
  institution = {{U.S. Election Assistance Commission}},
  address   = {Washington, DC},
  year      = {2025},
  url       = {https://www.eac.gov/research-and-data/studies-and-reports}
}"

what: Classify EAVS jurisdictions and derive county FIPS — classify_jurisdiction
title: Classify EAVS jurisdictions and derive county FIPS — classify_jurisdiction
text: EAVS reporting units vary across states: states report county level, others sub-county (township/municipality) level, report multi-county state-wide rows. helper adds columns needed roll EAVS file consistent county-level geography.

title: Classify EAVS jurisdictions and derive county FIPS — classify_jurisdiction
Classify EAVS jurisdictions and derive county FIPS — classify_jurisdiction
text: df EAVS data frame. ct_lookup Optional override Connecticut town--region table. Defaults package's ct_planning_regions data. wi_lookup Optional override Wisconsin sub-county lookup. Defaults package's wi_subcounty_fips data; data unavailable, WI sub-county rows receive NA county FIPS.

title: Classify EAVS jurisdictions and derive county FIPS — classify_jurisdiction
text: input data frame four columns added.

title: Classify EAVS jurisdictions and derive county FIPS — classify_jurisdiction
text: Columns added df: is_multi_county Logical; jurisdiction covers multiple counties. jurisdiction_type One "State", "Multi-county", "County", "Sub-county". state_fips Two-character state FIPS extracted fips_code. county_fips Five-character county-equivalent FIPS, Connecticut planning regions Wisconsin sub-county lookups applied possible. NA state multi-county rows. Input expectations: data frame already lower-cased column names (returned read_eavs(level = "jurisdiction") janitor::clean_names()), including fips_code, jurisdiction_name, state_abbr.

title: Classify EAVS jurisdictions and derive county FIPS — classify_jurisdiction
Cross-cycle EAVS variable crosswalk — crosswalk
text: hand-curated mapping raw EAVS variable codes stable harmonized names, topical section labels. Maintained data-raw/crosswalk.csv; see data-raw/build_crosswalk.R regeneration script.

title: Cross-cycle EAVS variable crosswalk — crosswalk
Cross-cycle EAVS variable crosswalk — crosswalk
text: tibble columns: cycle Integer EAVS cycle (e.g., 2024). variable Raw EAVS variable code released EAC. harmonized Stable cross-cycle variable name used eavsr. section Topical section (registration, technology, mail_voting, polling_places, staffing, rejections, participation). description Short human-readable description.

title: Connecticut town-to-planning-region lookup — ct_planning_regions
text: Maps Connecticut town names FIPS code Council Governments / Planning Region (county-equivalent geography federally recognized CT since 2022).

title: Connecticut town-to-planning-region lookup — ct_planning_regions
Connecticut town-to-planning-region lookup — ct_planning_regions
text: tibble columns town_name (uppercase), planning_region_code (5-character FIPS), planning_region_name.

title: Connecticut town-to-planning-region lookup — ct_planning_regions
text: Covers 169 Connecticut towns across 9 planning regions, built U.S. Census Bureau Gazetteer (2024 vintage) via data-raw/build_ct_planning_regions.R.

title: Download an EAVS cycle — download_eavs
text: Downloads public-release data file (optionally codebook) given EAVS cycle U.S. Election Assistance Commission caches file locally. Subsequent calls return cached path without re-downloading unless overwrite = TRUE.

title: Download an EAVS cycle — download_eavs
code: download_eavs(   cycle,   what = c("both", "data", "codebook"),   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Download an EAVS cycle — download_eavs
text: cycle Integer year EAVS cycle (e.g., 2024). file(s) download: "data", "codebook", "" (default). overwrite Logical. TRUE, re-download even cached copy exists. Defaults FALSE. quiet Logical. TRUE, suppress progress messages.

title: Download an EAVS cycle — download_eavs
text: character vector local file paths (named ).

title: Download an EAVS cycle — download_eavs

Local cache directory for EAVS files — eavs_cache_dir

title: Local cache directory for EAVS files — eavs_cache_dir

title: Inspect or clear the EAVS cache — eavs_cache_info
text: eavs_cache_info() returns tibble files currently cached, sizes modification times. eavs_clear_cache() removes cached files (optionally restricted single cycle).

title: Inspect or clear the EAVS cache — eavs_cache_info
Inspect or clear the EAVS cache — eavs_cache_info
text: cycle Optional integer cycle restrict .

title: Inspect or clear the EAVS cache — eavs_cache_info
text: eavs_cache_info() returns tibble; eavs_clear_cache() returns number files removed, invisibly.

title: Read an EAVS codebook — eavs_codebook
text: Returns EAVS codebook given cycle tibble, one row per variable. Downloads codebook file EAC already cached. Requires readxl package (installed automatically janitor's suggested deps via install.packages("readxl")).

title: Read an EAVS codebook — eavs_codebook
Read an EAVS codebook — eavs_codebook
text: cycle Integer year EAVS cycle.

title: Read an EAVS codebook — eavs_codebook
text: tibble codebook entries.

title: Read an EAVS codebook — eavs_codebook
Cross-cycle variable crosswalk — eavs_crosswalk
text: Returns package's hand-curated crosswalk maps EAVS variable codes (e.g. A1a, D2a, F1b) stable harmonized name across survey cycles, topical section label.

title: Cross-cycle variable crosswalk — eavs_crosswalk
Cross-cycle variable crosswalk — eavs_crosswalk
text: tibble one row per (cycle, variable) pair.

title: Cross-cycle variable crosswalk — eavs_crosswalk
text: intellectual core eavsr: EAVS variable names drift across cycles, analyses spanning multiple cycles need stable rename. shipped crosswalk starting point — open issue PR spot misalignment.

title: Cross-cycle variable crosswalk — eavs_crosswalk

what: eavsr: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey — eavsr-package
title: eavsr: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey — eavsr-package
text: Downloads, caches, harmonizes public-release files U.S. Election Assistance Commission's Election Administration Voting Survey (EAVS). Provides tidy access multiple survey cycles, exposes codebook tibble, supports joins county-level rurality classifications via rurality package.

title: eavsr: Tidy Access to the Election Administration and Voting Survey — eavsr-package
text: Maintainer: Cameron Wimpy cwimpy@astate.edu (ORCID) Authors: Cameron Wimpy cwimpy@astate.edu (ORCID)

what: Read an EAVS cycle as a tidy tibble — read_eavs
title: Read an EAVS cycle as a tidy tibble — read_eavs
text: Reads cached EAVS public-release file R tibble, EAVS missing-value codes (negative integers) recoded NA. Downloads file first already cached.

title: Read an EAVS cycle as a tidy tibble — read_eavs
code: read_eavs(   cycle,   level = c("jurisdiction", "raw"),   clean_names = NULL,   recode_missing = NULL )

title: Read an EAVS cycle as a tidy tibble — read_eavs
text: cycle Integer year EAVS cycle. level Aggregation level: "jurisdiction" (default, one row per reporting jurisdiction released EAC) "raw" (file exactly released, recoding). clean_names Logical. TRUE (default level = "jurisdiction"), apply janitor::clean_names() column names. recode_missing Logical. TRUE (default), recode negative numeric values NA. EAVS uses negative codes (-99, -77, etc.) indicate non-response missing data.

title: Read an EAVS cycle as a tidy tibble — read_eavs
text: tibble.

title: Read an EAVS cycle as a tidy tibble — read_eavs

what: Wisconsin sub-county to county FIPS lookup — wi_subcounty_fips
title: Wisconsin sub-county to county FIPS lookup — wi_subcounty_fips
text: Wisconsin reports EAVS sub-county (municipality) level. lookup links WI county FIPS code name variants (St./St-/etc.) support fuzzy matching messy jurisdiction_name strings.

title: Wisconsin sub-county to county FIPS lookup — wi_subcounty_fips
Wisconsin sub-county to county FIPS lookup — wi_subcounty_fips
text: tibble county_fips (5-character FIPS), county_name, four uppercased name-variant columns used matching.

title: Wisconsin sub-county to county FIPS lookup — wi_subcounty_fips
text: Covers 72 Wisconsin counties, built rurality package via data-raw/build_wi_subcounty.R. resolves ~97% WI EAVS rows; remainder municipalities spanning multiple counties (jurisdiction names containing "MULTIPLE COUNTIES"), flagged is_multi_county receive NA county FIPS design.

title: eavsr 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: eavsr 0.1.0
text: Package structure created DESCRIPTION, LICENSE, LICENSE.md, NAMESPACE, roxygen-based documentation. Core functions: eavs_cycles(), download_eavs(), read_eavs(), eavs_codebook(), eavs_crosswalk(), cite_eavs(). Cache management: eavs_cache_dir(), eavs_cache_info(), eavs_clear_cache(). 2024 cycle supported verified EAC URLs; earlier cycles (2004–2022) registered “planned” pending URL verification. Hand-curated cross-cycle variable crosswalk data-raw/crosswalk.csv. testthat suite covering cycle validation, cache behavior, citations, missing-value recoding. GitHub Actions workflows R CMD check pkgdown.

title: eavsr 0.1.0
text: classify_jurisdiction() adds jurisdiction_type, is_multi_county, state_fips, county_fips columns. Handles 2/5/10/11-character raw FIPS widths, Connecticut planning regions, Wisconsin sub-county name lookups. aggregate_to_county() combines direct-county rows summed sub-county rows, configurable mode-aggregated columns (default d8), customizable numeric summarizer, territory/Alaska drops. Two shipped lookup datasets: ct_planning_regions (partial — see ?ct_planning_regions) wi_subcounty_fips (built rurality package).
